sentence: The system shall provide best practice configurable workflow templates allow for configuration and automation of the common types of events and processes to be performed in the land development lifecycle application intake and processing inspections plan reviews etc.
The system will allow for the set up of process workflows to include endtoend process steps and milestones.

The system shall provide data validation rules to ensure data validity at the time of entry prevent an alpha character to be entered into a field that is configured to have only numeric values entered by the user.
The system shall provide the ability to configure custom workflows including but not limited to by type of application permit document plan inspection etc.
The system shall have the ability to support workflow task triggers scheduled timebased condition based manual etc.
The system shall have the ability to support workflow task routing scheduled timebased condition based manual etc.
The system will display all completed workflow tasks and related workflow data timestamp username in the System to provide a comprehensive view of activity taken on a record.

The system will display all uncompleted workflow tasks and related workflow data due date assigned user in the System to provide a comprehensive view of pending activity that will occur on a record for processing according to the associated workflow.

The system shall support reporting on workflowrelated transactions transaction volume response time amount of time a workflow step was in the users control operational reporting due dates by date range pastdue.
The System shall allow clocks for a workflow task to stop and start according to business rules.

The system shall allow authorized users supervisor to override and modify any workflow task progression in the system at any time if a Plan Reviewer selects Approved when the plans should have been disapproved authorized personnel such as a Supervisor should be able to change the status of the Plan Review or any final decision and move an application backwards in the workflow if it was advanced in error.
The system shall maintain an audit trail of workflow overrides record type changes business rule modifications and any other activity that is outside of standard practices.

The system should allow an authorized user to modify or otherwise correct the record type or other pertinent data associated with a record that is impacting its workflow processing rules to provide the correct information including handling all subordinatesecondary workflows and data changes that result from the original activityif a plan was initially categorized under the wrong plan type.
The system shall allow authorized users supervisor to manually override existing holds on applications according to predefined business rules
All subordinatesecondary workflows or data changes that result from the original activity must be handled.

The system shall allow an authorized user supervisor to edit the workflow history of a record to correct errors and handle all subordinatesecondary workflows and data changes that result from the original activity.
The system shall provide functionality to define specific performance parameters for all workflow tasks by application type inspection type plan review type etc. provided by the Agency.

The system shall allow an authorized business user nonIT personnel the ability to add andor change business rules in the system easily through the user interface according to user rolespermissions and all subordinatesecondary workflows and data changes that result from the original activity must be handled.
The system shall retain a revision history for business rule and workflow changes in the system including author type date and time of changes.
The system will have the ability to set an effective date for a business rule or workflow change.
The system will have the ability to set an expiration date for a business rule or workflow change.
The system shall allow multiple workflows at the same time for an individual process and allow users to manually override a workflow ie
To allow for grandfathering scenarios or implementation of new policies where certain applicationspermits need to follow a new business rule and others follow an existing process.
The system shall have the ability to associate workflow tasks to a role in the system workflow task is always assigned to a Site Plan Reviewer according to business rules configurable in the system.
The system shall have the ability to prevent or allow workflow task execution by a user according to the rolebased security assigned to the user in the system.
The system shall support individual user account work queue assignment workflow task is assigned to one individual.
The system shall support departmentbased work group assignment workflow task is assigned to the Zoning Administration Department.
The system shall support the automatic assignment of a workflow task to one or more users according to business rules and other factors employee skill sets availability locationdistricts.

The system shall support identification of reviewing agencies by pinpointing the affected property on a map and identifying responsible authorities planning districts zoning adjoining jurisdiction flood zone supervisor district etc. and support automatic assignment of the task to those users per business rules.

The system shall allow for multiple workflows to be configured in the system that differ across individual business areas according to their requirements workflow assignment rules differ by department.
The system shall allow authorized users to claim an active workflow task either assigned or unassigned for themselves and execute it immediately does not require an assignment or reassignment step.
The system shall support multiple tiers of assignment of reviews an application is first assigned to one individual who then forwards the application to others in that reviewing group to review.
The system shall support manual and automated reassignment of a workflow task from one user to another according to business rules.
The system shall support the manual or automated bulk reassignment of workflow tasks from one user to another according to business rules.
The system shall be able to capture the reason for reassignment
The system shall enforce a workflow routing list definition that supports if..then logic.
The system shall allow documents to be sent electronically according to defined routing lists and business rules as part of a workflow task or a communication notification.

The system shall provide the ability to designate a secondary approver who is authorized to access the queue and complete a task on behalf of a primary approver when the primary reviewer is unavailable Supervisors designee is authorized to approve an application.
The system shall provide the ability to incorporate checklists into the workflow process based on the transaction type andor record type plan review.
The system shall provide the ability to configure checklists at the role level each reviewer may have a distinct checklist per review task.
The system shall allow a reviewer to have their own workflow task with their own due date and timeline which may be different from other reviewers due date and checklist assigned to the same task.
The system shall provide the ability to perform automatic approval when transactions meet defined criteria some permits can be issued automatically via the online portal once payment is made.
The system shall have the ability to maintain query and display a list of tasks that have been assigned to a given user in the system Task list.
The system shall provide the ability for a user to view andor quickly access the last work list items the user was working on.
The system shall allow the adhoc sorting and filtering of their task list.
The system shall support configurable workflow task due dates based upon either calendar OR business days task is always due 10 days from task assignment response required within 2 business days.
The system shall support configurable workflow task durations.
The system shall support workflow task assignment rules that follow First In First Out FIFO approach.
The system shall allow a workflow task to be prioritized over other tasks in the work queue Supervisor flags a workflow task to be addressed first in a Plan Reviewers queue.
The system shall allow a workflow task to be expedited and the related due dates updated with the accelerated timeline.

The system shall allow application packages to be expedited by the Supervisor or System according to business rules for that record type or other trigger so that it is flagged is indicated by highlighting or appears first in the Reviewer s queue for expedited processing.
The system shall capture planned vacations or out of office information for staff to help inform assignments.
The system shall support workflow tasks that do not need to be assigned if a user dataenters an application he or she should not have to assign it to themselves to be able to process it.

The system shall support status changes for a record that is driven by the change in status of its underlying workflow tasks overall record status updates to Under Completeness Review once the Completeness Review task is assigned to a staff member.
The system shall support status changes for an individual workflow task.
The system shall support the automatic update of a status change for a workflow task.
The system shall support the manual update of a status change for a workflow task.

The system will support workflow associated with placing a hold on the record including preventing certain types of tasks to be executed in the system if a hold is on a particular record cannot issue a Certificate of Occupancy if there is a hold related to an open code enforcement case.
The system will enforce business rules associated with a hold type such as the conditions for triggering or removing a hold and the associated results of applying the hold in the system.
The system will enforce user permissions associated with a hold type only certain types of users may apply and remove holds.

The system shall have the ability to manually place or remove a hold condition on the record application project customer location that prevents a record from advancing in the workflow until the related issue is resolved.
The system shall have the ability to automatically place or remove a hold condition on the record application project customer location according to configured business rules in the system.
The system shall support multiple hold types with varying levels of severity and associated business rules.

The system should support hold types that may be related to preventing the issuance of a permit until all plan reviews have been logged as approved in the System only affects that particular application.
The system should support hold types that may be placed on the Applicant record that may affect all applications associated with that Applicant.
The system shall have the capability to apply multiple holds at one time put a hold on a contractor person record and all the projects associated with the contractor.

The system shall have the ability to support a scenario where there are multiple itemsissues that need to be addressed prior to release of the hold and they should be individualized holds which can be removed individually and only when the last item is completed will the hold be able to be lifted.

The system shall provide a structured template to capture conditions of the hold such as reasons for the hold instructions for the Customer and Staff on how and when to remove the hold including any documentsinformation that needs to be provided to release the hold and any internal notes or comments.
The system shall be able to populate the structured template to capture conditions of the hold with information already in the system such as the approved proffer language associated with the hold.
The system shall have the ability to publish any hold information to the customer portal as well as notify the customer and any other designated contacts of the hold based on business rules.
The system shall be able to notify designated Staff Members associated with the hold that action in the System has taken place on the affected record in order for them to manually remove the hold.
The system shall remove any workflow restrictions from the Customer s account andor related location property address Parcel Pin project etc. once the hold is lifted.
The system will allow review clocks to be stopped when a hold is placed and allow review clocks to be restarted when the hold is removed.

The system shall support configurable notifications email text message etc. that are triggered at various stages of workflow or other conditions according to business rules email generated to the Customer when their application is approved.

The system shall support configurable alerts visual indicators displayed in the system that are triggered according to business rules record turns red on the screen to indicate a workflow tasks duration has exceeded a predefined threshold.

The system shall support the capability to flag a property or user record manually or automatically according to business rules and send notifications to identified Agency Staff alerting them when a plan andor developmentpermitting application has been submitted for review.

The system shall provide configurable escalation capabilities if a workflow tasks duration has exceeded a predefined threshold the system will execute a predefined action such as generation of a Review Task for a Supervisor.

The system shall provide the ability to perform internal realtime message routing to broadcast information to a predefined group of users all assigned reviewers to a cancelled application are notified of the cancellation once it is processed in the system.
The system shall allow internal users to configure his or her notification settings would like to receive an email notification when a workflow task is assigned to him.
The system shall allow external users to configure his or her notification settings would like to receive an email when a status update occurs on their application.
The system shall allow staff to indicate the methods of communication email batch mailing user selected preference etc.
The system shall be able to send out one communication multiple ways both email and mail.
The system shall have the ability to send out mass emails according to business rules.
The system shall support the batch generation of communications for printing and mailing.
The system shall be able to track the date and time communications were sent from the system email mailings faxes etc. automatically.
The system shall be able to track the date and time communications were sent from the system email mailings faxes etc. that were performed manually.

The system will allow Staff to track undeliverable communication mail email etc. in the System and related activity attempts to resend the communication contacting the applicant to update his or her contact information changing applicants communication preferences in the system nonsystem generated correspondence.
The system shall be able to track returned mail email and letters including the datetime it was returned reason identified as why it was undeliverable etc.
The system shall be able to resend communications.
The system shall be able to track how many times a communication was sent or resent.
The system shall support transmittal of any information for large batch communication generation andor mailing batch generation of invoice notices
The system shall preserve a copy of the communications generated from the system email letters forms etc. and associate it with the relevant records.
The system should support Staff to be able to select recipients of the communication from stored contacts in the system .
The system shall have the capability to generate and manage multiple document template types including but not limited to the following Forms Letters Licenses Permits Certificates Emails.
The system shall have the capability to save drafts of generated documents as part of an automated work flow and manually by the user
The system shall allow multiple staff to access and collaborate on a document in the system.
The system shall track the users who edited the document and the datetime of changes.
The system shall be able to track whether a document has been sent to a Customer and datetime sent.
The system shall have the capability for staff to customize document templates edit a letters standard contents prior to sending to the Customer.
The system shall have the capability to automatically prepopulate templates with data from the system.
The system shall have the capability to allow staff to manually select data to populate templates from data sets in the system.
The system shall allow a user to upload documentation online via the customer portal.
The system shall allow staff to upload documentation submitted in paper format that has been scanned into the system.
The system shall allow personnel to upload files and attach them to a record in the system.
The system shall have the ability to upload multiple versions of the same document and maintain version control.
The system shall provide a way to differentiate between multiple versions of the same document through a naming or numbering convention configurable by the Agency.
The system should support uploading of documents for information purposes only .
The system shall capture metadata regarding the upload such as the date and time the documentation was uploaded.
The system shall have the capability to enforce or automatically generate document naming conventions.
The system shall provide staffdefined template fields to allow users to provide key metadata during the document upload process document name document description.
The system shall have the ability for a user to indicate the document type they are submitting.
The system shall have the ability for a user to indicate the document they are submitting is a new version of a previously submitted document.
The system shall allow a user to indicate they are uploading a component of larger package uploading 1 sheet that is part of a larger plan set.
The system shall have the ability to associate documentation with one or more records in the system according to business rules an application a project a transaction etc.
The system shall have the ability to display uploaded documentation or plans in a thumbnail or similar view format.
The system shall provide an option for the user to view all uploaded files prior to formal submission to ensure quality of upload.
The system shall provide a mechanism for the user to track the inprogress status of the document upload upload window that incrementally increases based on percentage complete of the upload.
The system shall have the capability to export documents and reports from the system print fax email.
The system must retain the document s scale when it is printed.
The system must retain the document s scale when it is vieweddisplayed in the system.
The system shall be able to generate documents such as receipts permits licenses letters certificates etc. that can be posted to the customer portal and available for printing by the user.

The system shall support the automatic identification of records of project information plans and other supporting documents according to the record retention policies configured in the system that may be eligible for archiving or deletion.

The system shall allow for the automatic deletion of project information plans and other supporting documents according to the record retention policies configured in the system or as deemed appropriate by the agencys business process.
The system shall have the capability to search for a document by document attributes document name date sent related application etc.
The system shall have the ability to overlay documents with watermarks.
The system shall have the ability to overlay documents with text Approved .
The system shall support electronic signature via the online portal click to sign initial and date indicates electronic signature.
The system links the electronic signature to the documents being signed if appropriate.
The system can apply an electronic signature obtained out in the field via a mobile device to the document they are signing property owner signs the inspection report out in the field electronically.
The system shall have the capability to capture electronic certification agreement with terms and conditions.
The system shall have the ability to capture seals authorized inspector seal electronically.
The system shall support electronic notarization.
The system shall have the ability to visually indicate to a supervisor if performance metrics are approaching or have surpassed configured targets.
The system shall allow a Supervisor to view all staff previously assigned to a projectapplication.
The system shall allow the Supervisor to drill down into specific sections of the project as necessary to view details associated with the project inspections.
The system shall display the application processor workload schedules supported through any necessary 3rd party calendaring systems like Google Calendar or Microsoft Outlook.

The system shall provide electronic notifications to the Supervisor before and when predefined performance parameters are exceeded on certain applications or when notable events occur on certain applicationsplans.
The system shall be able to determine a staff members availability through the combination and calculation of multiple factors example of criteria below.
Expected duration of assigned tasks.
Availability indicated on a calendar.
The System shall provide a smart calendar to forecast dates for timelines and assist the Supervisor in assigning workloads accordingly.

The system shall provide a wide range of adhoc searching capabilities to support business needs such as searching at the project license application location or person level according to various attributes and multiple search criteria.
The system shall provide the ability to search all data in the system by keyword
Searches must not be case sensitive.

The system shall support robust search capabilities for Staff and Customers searching zoning ordinance and proffer interpretation requests zoning compliance letters and additional time requests keyword search location searches version searches etc.
The system shall support robust search capabilities for Staff and Customers searching plan ordinance information keyword search location searches version searches etc.
The system shall support sorting of displayed records based on their attributes.
The system shall support filtering of displayed records based on their attributes.
The system shall be able to save a search as a template to easily retrieve and execute in the future.
The system shall be able to export search results in Microsoft Excel format.
The system shall be able to export search results in .pdf format.
The system shall be able to save search results in the system.

The system shall prevent the user from executing a large query or report that will exceed the predefined threshold for the number of results returned that may impact system performance if the report is run except by authorized users administrators and report servers.
The system shall provide options for suggested searches if a search does not return a search result.

The system shall be able to provide contact information for the related Agency office if a search does not return a search result if user is searching the customer portal for a particular topic such as building inspections the system shall be able to provide contact information for the building inspections department.
The system will have the ability to create a user name and temporary user password when a Customer account is created.
The system will send a email to the new user account using the email entered into the new user account that the account is ready for activation.
The system will be able to include the users user name and temporary password in the activation email.
The system will be able to include an activation link in the activation email.
The system shall flag inactive accounts according to business rules.
An authorized user shall have the ability to deactivate andor suspend user accounts.
An authorized user shall have the ability to create and modify user accounts
The system shall prevent the creation of an account according to predefined business rules account already exists for that individual.
The system shall provide a mechanism where an internal or external user can reset their forgotten password.
The system will allow for staff to merge records to prevent duplicate Customer accounts.
The system shall support a GIScentric approach to tracking locationspecific planning zoning permitting development and code violation activities in the system.

The system shall support a GIScentric approach to address management leverage a GIS system to organize all locationbased information including but not limited to addresses Parcel pin number proffers etc.
The system shall be able to validate information captured in the System or provided by an Applicant against the Agencys GIS Central Data Repository address is located within Agency.

The system shall be able to validate the eligibility of a transaction in the system according to business rules based on GIS data environmental constraints while allowing authorized users the ability to override.

The system shall be able to trigger workflow tasks according to business rules configured in the system based on GIS data trigger required Agency andor departmental reviews based on the geographical boundaries.
The system shall be able to generate and print custom reports from the system that include GIS data and mappings.
The system will support the addressing of locations.
The system shall be able to track location information in the system using the unique identifier for the address.
The system shall be able to associate filesdocuments with an address pin legal description and other parcel identifiers including nonaddressed locations such as easement proffer
The system shall be able to track location by one or more components of a secondary address suite apartment building floor etc.
The system shall be able to track the legal description associated with a location by the Assessors Office see Interface requirements.
The system shall be able to track information in the system by County PIN numbers parcel identification numbers based on geographic location or a polygon indicated by the user
The system shall be able to track information in the system by GIS Points eg addressstreet intersection points.
The system shall be able to support tracking parcel information by the Agencys Parcel Pin Numbers which include leading 0s in the identifier
The system should recognize and remove embedded spaces or hyphens input by the user and reduce pin to just numerics.
The system shall be able to track location information as longitude and latitude.
The system shall be able to track location information as a GIS pin on a map.
The system shall be able to allow staff to indicate when the mailing address for an application differs from the actual onsite location associated with the permitted activity.
The system shall be able to track information as a point address point intersection of latlong etc.
The system shall be able to track information as a line or lines road sections rightofway etc.
The system shall support tracking location information on areas outside of the Agency as needed to support business operations.
The system shall be able to track the location of fire lanes.
The system shall be able to track records in the Right of Way address point permit easement etc.
The system shall be able to track easements.
The system shall be able to track the location of septic systems and wells for the Health Department to issue septic system and well permits.
The system shall be able to track government state federal facility addresses.
The system shall be able to track multiple addresses on a parcel OR a building including suites and track an item at any address level.
The system shall support the creation of addressing information in the System including addresses for areas outside of the Agency.
The system shall support the temporary addressing of a location according to business rules by Parcel pin.
The system shall prevent workflow from advancing to a certain point according to business rules if a location is not assigned a valid parcel number and address.
The system shall provide a mechanism to track nonaddressable locations in the system.
The system shall allow issuance of a permit that does not require an exact address retaining wall.
The system shall capture location information that is not an address but can help inform the location of permitted work for inspectors notes related to the location.
The system shall be able to track a location with multiple address points and one Parcel pin number.
The system shall be able to track a location based on the level in the mall upper level.
The system shall be able to track the history of addressing for a location.
The system shall be able to track parent and child relationships for parcels and addresses provided by the GIS system.

The system shall be able to support the change in Parcel pin numbers through an interface with GIS Parcel pin numbers change when a property is subdivided and the new parcels created are assigned a new Parcel pin number.
The system shall be able to provide a suggested address if the address provided by an Applicant does not have an exact match in the system.
The system shall support the change of address for a location in the system.
The system will track any change in sizes and locations of buildings and suites.
The system will preserve the history of a building or suite what it used to be versus what it is now.
The system shall support the processing of a request for a change of address and track all related information.
The system shall trigger any associated workflow activities related to a change of address including required reviews inspections and fees and submissiontracking of the board package submission.
The system shall support the change of a street name in the system.

The system shall be able to provide a workflow and track requests for information and staff responses about addressing address verification requests suite numbering questions availableeligible street names for a subdivision.
The system shall be able to store notificationsfiles from the Courthouse or the Commissioner of the Revenue related to new addresses that need to be updated in the system.

The system shall prevent duplicate addresses street names and subdivisions from being added to the system through the COR interface if a new address was manually entered into the system during the day and the mapping address registry interface runs at night the system will prevent both the manually entered address and the address generated from the interface to be in the system at the same time.
The System shall provide Tool Tips whenever feasible providing messages andor assistance as the user hovers over elements of the graphical interface.
The system shall have the ability to trigger all required downstream workflow related to the issuance of a permit such as inspections configurable by permit type.
The system shall provide a robust online portal capable of processing all online application types and their associated workflow and business rules.
The system shall have the ability to configure all necessary application content data entry fields help text submittal requirements etc. on all screens in the system.
The system shall be able to clearly indicate which fields were completed by a Customer and which fields were completed by Staff in the System.
The system shall clearly indicate which fields must be completed before the user can advance to the next screen.
The system shall have the ability to enforce the creation of a user account prior to specific transactions being performed online.
The system shall have the capability to allow specific transactions to be performed without a user account.
The system shall allow users to search data in the system that has been flagged as displayable to the public via the customer portal without being logged in.
The system shall allow users to view GIS maps without having the user being logged into the GIS.
The system shall allow users to execute queries related to GIS data search Zoning Map without having the user being logged into the GIS.
The system shall allow authorized users to search proffer data in the system proffer description milestonestriggers after logging in.
The system shall allow users to view information related to departmental news and other updates that are posted to the customer portal without being logged in a news ribbon or bulletin board.
The system shall provide external links to Frequently Asked Questions FAQ pages that the customer can select without being logged in.
The system shall provide Contact Us links that the user can select to submit an electronic message to Agency staff without being logged in.
The system shall provide a standard template for submitting a Contact Us request to assist support in the categorization and routing of these messages to relevant Agency staff.

The system shall provide robust query options for nonlogged in users to search publicly available information stored in the system research on Agency parcels ordinances departmental processes and services application submission requirements as well as data stored in the System that is public information such as approved plan packages issued permits complaint cases etc.
The system shall provide an option for users to search for and view projectspecific information without being logged in application status plan review status plan review comments.

The system shall provide an option for users to search for a particular location to see active applications for rezonings permits etc. in a designated geographical area on an interactive map that displays active projects Whats going on in my neighborhood.
The system shall return relevant data that matches search criteria entered by the user via the online portal additional searching and reporting requirements are included in the General Tab.

The system shall allow the data returned in the online portal searches to be exported out of the system downloaded in Excel format if request is allowable not all information requests should be downloaded.
The system shall allow the data returned in the online portal searches to be printed from the system if request is allowable not all information requests should be printed.

The System shall be able to generate a popup window that surveys the Customer at defined points in the lifecycle of their project according to Agency business rules on their experience and provide a section for customer comments.
The system shall support submission of a Freedom of Information Act FOIA request that is supported through configurable workflow and business rules for processing by staff in the system.
The system shall provide appropriate online help features chat email phone contact information at all points in the application lifecycle.

The system shall provide an online tool that is available to customers without logging in that will provide an interactive experience for the Customer providing both general information as well as information tailored to the Customer s needs that will guide them through the application process Select a Service wizard.

The systems Select a Service wizard shall be configured with a decision tree and other wizardlike functionality that allows prospective applicants to select their services based on a stepbystep smartly configured collection of data based on the project service or application that the Customer is interested in.

The system shall have the ability to prompt a user to respond to a series of questions to guide an applicant to select the appropriate service offering provided by the Agency that is similar to the questions that Intake Staff would ask a Customer in person.

The system shall present a structured and guided series of questions that reflect a predetermined decision tree to assist the user in selecting a service turbo tax style prompts and formfill features.
The system shall allow the user to provide specific information related to their project based on the questions asked by the wizard tool.
The system shall allow the user to indicate the location of their project including parcel address or GIS coordinates or select the location from a map.
The system shall retrieve relevant and publicly displayable locationbased data that could inform the Select a Service wizards responses.
The system shall retrieve relevant data to guide the user to understand Comprehensive Plan information relevant to their project as part of the Select a Service wizard.
The system shall retrieve zoning information relevant to the users project as part of the Select a Service wizard.

The system shall retrieve proffers and development conditions as well as any relevant information such as documentation andor a case number relevant to their project as part of the Select a Service wizard.
The system shall retrieve publicly available existing site and subdivision dataplans relevant to their project as part of the Select a Service wizard.
The system shall allow an authorized user to retrieve existing permits relevant to their projects as part of the Select a Service wizard.
The system shall allow an authorized user to retrieve existing occupancy permits relevant to their projects as part of the Select a Service wizard.
The system shall retrieve existing use information relevant to their project as part of the Select a Service wizard.
The system shall retrieve existing code enforcement case information that is designated for public viewing relevant to their project as part of the Select a Service wizard.

The system shall present a structured and guided series of questions that can provide a dynamic and interactive Frequently Asked Questions FAQ tool where detailed information can be provided to the user such as an overview of processes and submission requirements and tips based on the data entered andor options selected by the user.

The system shall notify the applicant if the information entered does not return any results for services or if the specifics of the project exceed the complexity the wizard can handle and provide information on how to contact Intake Staff for further assistance.

The system shall provide a mechanism for the user to submit a request to Agency staff for manual handling if the information entered does not return any results for services or if the specifics of the project exceed the complexity the wizard can handle.
The system shall provide a structured template for a user to submit a request for Agency assistance prompt the user to log in or enter contact information.
The system may provide a hyperlink to the user that directs them to a department webpage FAQ page or other external site that provides detailed information regarding their query.
The system shall provide a final set of information that answers the Customer s question andor directs them to an appropriate application type they should submit
suggests one or more recommended services application types or other online transaction options based on information entered by the applicant.

The system shall provide an option for the user to initiate the application process directly from the Select a Service wizard tool and launch the appropriate application form from the Select a Service wizard tool user should not have to remember the application type recommended by the wizard and select the form to apply for again as part of the application process.
The system shall prepopulate the launched application form with any information entered into the Select a Service wizard that also appears on the application form project location.
The system shall prompt the user to log in to the system when initiating the application process.

The system shall be able to provide an overview of the steps in the application process for the recommended application type such as submission requirements departments involved expected timelines and associated fees.
The system shall be able to notify the user that preapplication assistance is available or recommended as an option for the application type that has been recommended.

The system shall allow the user to advance or return to various pages in the wizard tool to explore different decision tree paths be able to go back to a previous page and select a different answer which triggers a different series of questions.
The system shall provide an option for the user to exit the wizard without electing to initiate the application process.

The system shall allow a user to proceed directly to the application submission process without using the wizard in no case shall the structured process hinder an advanced user to directly and quickly interact with the Agency.

The system shall be able to prompt the user to indicate whether the application is for a location or address already stored in the user profile if the user is logged in while using the wizard tool so that the user does not have to reenter location information.
The system shall provide a mechanism to allow Staff to log phone calls and walkins and associated information property or location permit number application number requestor information.

The system shall be able to track trends related to customer inquiries common questions that could be used to inform the information posted online track activity on a particular parcel track questions asked and responses provided to help ensure consistency in Agency responses etc.
The system shall provide website analytics which allow the Agency to track customer behavior on the customer portal.
The system shall provide an ability to track searches performed by the users on the customer portal to track trends.
The system shall support the online intake of requests for preapplication assistance from registered users of the customer portal.
The system shall only support preapplication assistance workflow for eligible application types.

The system shall provide a structured configurable template including an option to upload documents for a user to submit a request for preapplication assistance that contains all the information needed for Staff to understand the proposed project and evaluate the request for the meeting.
The system shall support the maintenance of multiple preapplication meeting calendars to track staff availability and existing approved meetings to assist in scheduling.
The system shall support rescheduling of preapplication meetings via the calendar.
The system shall support canceling of preapplication meetings via the calendar.
The system shall allow users to select a preferred not guaranteed date and time online for the preapplication meeting using the calendar to view available timeslots.
The system shall provide a checklistFAQs to the user to assist in determining if the user is ready to schedule a preapplication meeting or to notify them of prerequisites for the meeting.
The system shall allow staff to manually log a request for preapplication in the system.

The THEMAS system shall control the heating and cooling units that are defined as part of the THEMAS system.

There shall be a maximum number of heating or cooling u nits that can be on at any given time.
The system shall be able to use trigger values in combination with current temperature to determine heating or cooling activation.
The trigger values shall reside in an initialization file.
The THEMAS system shall ensure the temperature reported by a given thermostat shall not exceed a maximum deviation value of 3 degrees Fahrenheit.
The over temperature values shall reside in an initialization file.
Upon initialization the THEMAS system shall be able to automatically turn off all heating and cooling units.

The THEMAS system shall be able to accept temperature readings from thermostats only within a predefined valid range.

The THEMAS system shall be able to monitor temperature by comparing reported values to settings and detecting any exceeding of specified limits.
The system shall be able to output the temperature status when the measured temperature T is within the acceptable range defined by the lower limit LO and upper limit UO.
The system shall be able to activate the appropriate heating or cooling unit based on a predefined temperature differential between the current temperature and setting.
The THEMAS system shall be able to control all defined heating and cooling units.
The THEMAS system shall be able to generate and record an event when a heating or cooling request is denied.
The system shall be able to monitor thermostat and heatingcooling data to trigger specific events.
The THEMAS system shall control the heating and cooling units that are designated as part of the THEMAS system.
The system shall be able to translate user requests to turn onoff heating or cooling units into corresponding control commands.
Once a request to turn on or off a heating or cooling unit the THEMAS system shall provide the necessary control signal for the unit.
The system shall be able to interpret desired heatingcooling unit and its requested ONOFF status.
When an event occurs the THEMAS system shall identify the event type and format an appropriate event message.

The automated diagnostic tool shall input sensed data in real time from data acquisition devices and the HVAC unit under diagnosis as well as from data files containing previously acquired data though not simultaneously.
The tool shall input sensed data in real time from data acquisition devices and the HVAC unit under diagnosis.
The tool shall permit sensed data to be input from an electronic file though not simultaneously with sensed data from external acquisition hardware.
The system shall qualify the certainty of each sensed data item during acquisition flagging doubtful data due to errors or unexpected values.
The system shall identify potentially faulty sensor data based on a sensorspecific number of consecutive missed readings due to communication issues.
Sensed data used in or supporting diagnostic analyses shall be saved to permanent storage.

The occurrence of missing data shall be indicated in permanent storage by a unique and obvious place holder or indicator a null value or sequence of characters that cannot be confused with nonmissing data.
Sensed data input to the tool shall be stored to permanent storage with a precision of three significant figures.
The administrator shall have the capability to modify the expected range of sensed data against which the data will be validated.
The tool shall associate with each continuous parameter one or more tolerances each corresponding to a different sensitivity level for the tool.

Any and all errors associated with obtaining validating converting or storing the sensed data shall be logged as described in subsequent specifications and selected errors shall be reported to the user.

The tool shall monitor the unit under diagnosis either in real time or batch mode for an indefinite period of time or until input data are no longer available and log diagnostic results as they are determined.

Diagnostic results that are saved to permanent storage shall be accompanied in storage by the absolute time of occurrence the identity of the unit to which the diagnostic applies and the identity of the diagnostic.
The administrator user shall have the capability to adjust the sensitivity of the diagnostic analysis in order to increase or decrease the probability of fault detection.
The automated diagnostic tool shall display and record diagnostic results
Diagnostic results to be displayed to the user will be done so in a simple graphical and textual format.
The administrator shall have the ability to specify which diagnostic results will not be reported in any manner not detected.
The system shall recognize user selection by mouse across the entire width of an item including its diagnostic indicators in main systems and condition windows.
The windows listing diagnostic results shall increase in height as needed to display entries up to a maximum of ten entries.
The building subsystems and condition windows shall display the current time if the diagnostic tool is operating in real time.

If only a single building is defined the subsystems window shall become the main window of the application and shall have all the functionality of the main window as described in these specifications.
Each window of the diagnostic tool shall display an animated graphical feature indicating the status whether active or not of the diagnostic processing.

The subsystem window shall be modal to the building window the condition window modal to the subsystem window and the diagnostic history and diagnostic information windows shall be modal to the condition window.
Significant errors in software operation shall be logged to permanent storage with sufficient detail to guide the administrator and to assist support staff in locating and rectifying the error.

Software errors logged to permanent storage shall include the absolute time of occurrence the name of the software module in which the error occurred the name of the software function in which the error occurred and a description of the error.
The user interface shall consist of six windows Main window configuration window subsystems window condition window diagnostic information window and diagnostic history window.
The main window shall list buildings monitored and summarizing the current diagnostic status of all contained monitored units.
The main window shall have main menus entitled File and Status.
The File menu shall offer Configure Diagnostics Logout and Exit in that order.
The system shall offer a Status main menu with Start Diagnostics and Stop Diagnostics in that order.
The menu items named Configure Diagnostics and Exit in the main menu entitled File shall be enabled at all times
The system shall only enable the Logout menu item in the File menu if the current user is authenticated as the administrator.
The Start Diagnostics menu item in the Status menu shall be enabled only when diagnostics arent running otherwise it shall be disabled.
The Stop Diagnostics menu item in the Status menu shall be enabled only when diagnostics are running and disabled otherwise.
Selecting Configure Diagnostics from the File menu shall open the configuration window modally making it the active window in front of the main window.

The configuration window shall have a button labeled Save that when clicked will present the user with a window by which to enter a disk file or database name in which to save the modifiable data in the window.
The configuration window shall have a button labeled Close that will dismiss the configuration window and return the user to the main window.
The configuration window shall have a button labeled Recall that will permit the user to recall a disk file of previously saved parameters from which to populate the configuration window.
The subsystems window shall list subsystems monitored for a specific building and summarizing the current diagnostic status of all monitored subsystems.

The diagnostic information window shall have a button labeled Print that when clicked will display a window listing items to print including the diagnostic information window the history window and both the diagnostic and history windows.
The diagnostic information window shall have a button labeled Close that shall dismiss the window.
The diagnostic information window shall have a button labeled History that when clicked will display the diagnostic history window and display the previous weeks diagnostic results.
The diagnostic history window shall display historical diagnostic results for a specific diagnostic.
The diagnostic history window shall provide user selections for start times and end times for the history interval.
The diagnostic history window shall display data in intervals of one week or greater.
The diagnostic history window shall display a maximum time interval of two years.

The color map of the history window shall indicate confirmed faults with the color red potential faults with the color yellow normal conditions with the color green and periods when diagnostic processing was not active with the color gray.
The vertical axis of the color map of the history window shall indicate hour of the day and the horizontal axis shall indicate the absolute date and the day of week.

The diagnostic history window shall have a button labeled Print that when clicked will display a window listing items to print including the diagnostic information window the history window and both the diagnostic and history windows.
The diagnostic history window shall have a button labeled Close that will dismiss the window.
The subsystems window condition window and diagnostic information window shall display a title that indicates its specific location or absolute path in the navigation hierarchy of windows
The main window subsystems window and condition window shall have three diagnostic indicators for each item listed.
An OK indicator appearing green shall signify the absence of any diagnostic conditions.
A yellow Caution indicator will be used to signify potential diagnostic issues.
A red Not OK indicator will be active to signify a confirmed diagnostic condition.
Only one indicator shall be active at a given time for a specific diagnostic and no indicators shall flash when active.
The diagnostic indicators on the main window and subsystems window shall summarize all diagnostic conditions associated with each entry in the window.
The tool shall keep a permanent record of any modifications made to fixed data.
The log shall indicate the time and date of the change original value of the items hanged and the new value of the items
The game shall only display the current block positions and not positions from canceled movements.

The client will send a GET request to the Web API with the question as a URL parameter.
The client will specify the header ContentType applicationjson in their requests as convention.
An API query shall be a single URL parameter formulated as a single clear question in standard English.
The client will be able to parse the JSON and determine if there was an error.
The server will send all API data in JSON response documents with the header ContentType applicationjson.
The server will respond with a 200 OK status code if a request has the header ContentType applicationjson and is a valid API query.
The server will respond with a 400 Bad Request status code if request does not specify the header ContentType applicationjson OR is a malformed API query.
This unit will receive a text string from the URL parameter.
This unit will output the sentence as a string
A MySQL database will be used to store all information required to answer questions.
The information extraction unit shall populate the database before system startup to ensure immediate data accessibility.
The database will be updated periodically and the API will be unavailable during the update.
The GUI will have a textbox that will accept inputs from a keyboard.
Text box will originally contain a suggestive text question to guide the user to the format of an appropriate question.
The GUI will have a chat window displaying questions sent to the system and responses from the API.
The chat window will contain all questions and answers from the current session with a scroll bar if all messages cant fit on the screen.
If there is a network issue the chat window will display an error message.
There will be a designated phone number that users can send texts to.
Texts sent to that number will be sent to the API then the system will reply to the user with the answer as another text message.
If a question is not understood by our API the system will send a text containing an example question after the text with the API response.

System shall prevent external users from requesting original receipts restricting this action to internal users.
System shall provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of a ticket retrieval request including the dispute case number type of retrieval requested and due date for the merchant response.

The Disputes System shall allow users to create chargeback requests prompting for required information and resulting in printed notifications sent to merchants if proof of disputed transactions is not provided.
The Disputes System must provide confirmation upon chargeback request creation containing dispute case number type of chargeback requested and merchant response due date.
The Disputes System shall prevent creation of duplicate dispute requests.

The Disputes System must provide viewing capability for existing dispute cases.

The Disputes System must provide Search functionality allowing search by dispute case number merchant account number cardmember account number and issuer number with filtering options by date range dispute type case status and reason code.
A list of disputes meeting search criteria must be provided differentiating by colorcoded status.
The Disputes System shall provide users with the ability to view the history of all activities on dispute cases.
The Dispute System shall enable users to follow up on dispute cases filtering followup actions by case status and user access level.
The Disputes System shall record user names and dates for all caserelated activities maintaining detailed audit history.
For any systematic action such as disputes aging process a case activity will be generated and the user who initiated the dispute case must be notified upon next logon.
All letter requests must adhere to formatting guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
Any disputes cases closed for over 6 months must be purged from the online disputes database.
The user shall search for the preferred repair facility using vehicle location and radius in miles.
The vehicle data shall include vehicle year make and model.
The vehicle location shall include street address city state and zipcode.
The system shall resolve the zipcode for the street address city and state if zipcode is unknown.
The search radius shall be between 1 and 30 miles.
The system shall locate the preferred repair facility with the highest ratings for the input criteria.
The adjuster shall enter the preferred repair facility on the estimate assignment.
The adjuster shall be able to override the preferred repair facility on the estimate assignment.
The preferred repair facility shall be returned to the user.
The system shall return a list of repair facilities within the radius if the preferred repair facility cannot be determined.
The estimator shall search for available recycled parts using damaged vehicle parts information.
The estimator shall search for available recycled parts using a list of preferred parts suppliers.
The system shall search for available recycled parts for the supplied vehicle parts and suppliers.
The estimator shall search by zipcode with a radius of 30 miles if no parts are found for the preferred parts suppliers.
The system shall retain the available recycled parts and suppliers returned from the search.
The available recycled parts information and their suppliers shall be returned to the user.
The estimator shall apply selected recycled parts to the collision estimate.
The estimator shall not apply recycled parts to the collision estimate if no available parts are returned.
The adjuster shall review the collision estimate.
The adjuster shall request a recycled parts audit of the collision estimate.
The recycled parts search results provided to the estimator shall be retrieved by the system.
The system shall generate an audit report based on the available recycled parts and the collision estimate.
The audit report shall include the total number of recycled parts used in the estimate.
The audit report shall include the number of available recycled parts from the original search results.
The audit report shall include the percentage of available recycled parts used in the estimate.
The audit report shall include the total score of the audit which sums the individual line items.
The audit report shall be displayed to the user.
The audit report shall be available to other adjusters at later points in time.
The user shall select to view the preferred repair facility ratings.
The current repair facility ratings shall be displayed to the user.
The system will display a blank set of ratings if there are not ratings yet defined.
The user shall enter new ratings based on the audit report.
The ratings shall be from a scale of 1
The ratings shall include categories for attempted use of recycled parts and actual use of recycled parts.
The user shall select to save the preferred repair facility ratings.
The preferred repair facility ratings shall be saved by the system.

The Disputes System must be accessible by both internal and external users.
The Disputes System must prevent users from accessing any dispute cases that do not belong to their cardholder base.
The Disputes System will facilitate direct data entry of a dispute case via a user interface that supports real time responses to the users.
The Disputes System must provide different levels of access with regard to disputes case initiation and followup actions.
The Disputes System shall provide view access capability for authorized users of the application.
The Disputes System shall provide update access capability for authorized users of the application.

The Disputes System must allow the users to select disputable transactions based on the age of the transaction from a user interface and initiate a dispute ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification on the selected transaction.
The Disputes System must provide the user the ability to initiate a single dispute case on multiple transactions that belong to a single merchant.
The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request
As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request
The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
The Disputes System must allow the user to create three unique types of ticket retrieval requests
The three types of ticket retrieval requests are 1 Request for original receipt 2 Request for a copy of the receipt or 3 Request for a portfolio
A portfolio consists of documentation that would provide proof of a purchase such as the documentation that is received from a car rental agency that is more than a sales receipt.
The Disputes System must prevent external users from requesting original receipts
Requests for original receipts are restricted to internal users.

The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of ticket retrieval request that contains the following information the dispute case number the type of retrieval requested copy original or portfolio and the date that the merchant response is due.
The Disputes System shall allow the user to create or initiate a chargeback request
The system must prompt the user to enter the required information to initiate a chargeback request

The chargeback request results in a printed chargeback notification that is sent to merchant indicating that their account will be charged back if no proof is provided that the disputed transaction is valid.
The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of a chargeback request
The confirmation must contain the following information the dispute case number the type of chargeback requested pending or immediate and the date that the merchant response is due.
The Disputes System shall prevent the creation of duplicate dispute requests 100 of the time.
The Disputes System shall accurately provide a list of possible reason codes for a given transaction
The list of possible reason codes must be derived based on the type of transaction and the industry that the merchant belongs to according to the business rules defined for the Disputes System.
The Disputes System must provide the ability to view existing dispute cases.
The Disputes System must provide Search functionality
The search method must include the ability to search by 1 the dispute case number 2 the merchant account number 3 the cardmember account number and 4 the issuer number

In addition to the above criteria the search functionality must further allow the user to limit the results of the search by a date range the type of dispute ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification the case status open closed or all and the dispute reason code.
A list of all disputes cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user
The list of cases must differentiate the type andor status of the case by color coding the line item in the list
For example all open cases will be yellow and all closed cases will be gray.
The Disputes System shall provide the users the ability to view the history of all activities that have occurred on the dispute case.
The Dispute System shall provide the users with the ability to follow up on a dispute case via followup actions
The list of available follow up actions for a given dispute case must be filtered by the status of the case and the access level of the user.
The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system
A detailed history of the actions taken on the case including the date and the user that performed the action must be maintained for auditing purposes.
For any systematic nonuser initiated action that occurs on a case such as the disputes aging process a case activity will be generated
Upon the next logon the user that initiated the dispute case must be notified that a systematic action has occurred on the dispute case.
All letter requests must be formatted according to guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
Any disputes cases that have been closed for over 6 months must be purged from the online disputes database.

Once a game is initiated the product shall display a defensive grid to each player
Once a game is initiated the product shall display an offensive grid to each player.
The product shall prevent a player from viewing the offensive or defensive grids of the other player.
Once a game is initiated the product shall allow each player to position their 5 ships on their respective defensive grids.
The product shall allow a player to position ships horizontally on their grid.
The product shall allow the player to position ships vertically on their grid.
The product shall allow the player to position ships diagonally on their grid
The product shall prevent the player from overlapping ships on their grid.
When both players have positioned their 5 ships on their defensive grids the product shall define the status of each player for the first turn.
For each turn the product shall identify one player as offensive status and one player as defensive status.
The product shall maintain the status of each player for a turn either offensive or defensive.
At the start of each turn the product shall notify each player of his or her status.
For each turn the product shall allow the offensive player to define a shot.
For each shot the product shall examine the defensive grid of the defensive player to determine whether the shot was a hit or miss.
For each shot the product shall notify the players whether the shot was a hit or miss.
For each shot the product shall mark the shot as a hit or miss on the defensive grid of the defensive player.
For each shot the product shall mark the shot as a hit or miss on the offensive grid of the offensive player.
When the hit results in sinking a ship the product shall notify the players that the ship was sunk.
When the hit results in sinking a ship the product shall mark the ship as sunk on the defensive grid of the defensive player.
When the hit results in sinking a ship the product shall indicate the type of ship sunk on the defensive grid of the defensive player.
When the hit results in sinking a ship the product shall mark the ship as sunk on the offensive grid of the offensive player.
If the shot was marked as a hit the product shall allow the offensive player to define a shot.
If the shot was marked as a miss the product shall change the status of the offensive player to defensive
If the shot was marked as a miss the product shall change the status of the defensive player to offensive
The product shall continue to assign turns until the game is ended.
The product shall end a game when a hit by the offensive player results in sinking the last of the defensive player s ships which had not been previously marked as sunk.
The product shall allow a player to end a game at any time during the game.

Download mobile application DESC A user should be able to download the mobile application through either an application store or similar service on the mobile phone
The application should be free to download.
Download and notify users of new releases DESC When a newupdated version or release of the software is released the user should check for these manually
The download of the new release should be done through the mobile phone in the same way as downloading the mobile application.
User registration Mobile application DESC Given that a user has downloaded the mobile application then the user should be able to register through the mobile application
The user must provide username password and email address
The user can choose to provide a regularly used phone number.
User login Mobile application DESC Given that a user has registered then the user should be able to log in to the mobile application
The login information will be stored on the phone and in the future the user should be logged in automatically.
Retrieve password DESC Given that a user has registered then the user should be able to retrieve hisher password by email.
Mobile application Search DESC Given that a user is logged in to the mobile application then the first page that is shown should be the search page
The user should be able to search for a restaurant according to several search options
The search options are Price Destination Restaurant type and Specific dish
There should also be a free text search option
A user should be able to select multiple search options in one search.
Mobile application Search result in a map view DESC Search results can be viewed on a map
On the map the relevant and closest restaurants according to the user position are shown
A specific pin will represent a specific restaurant location
On each pin there should be an information link
There should be maximally 100 results displayed
The map view should have a default zoom
The map view should include a button that when selected should display different filtering options in a filtering menu.
Search result in a list view Search results can be viewed in a list
Each element in the list represents a specific restaurant

Each element should include the restaurant name telephone number type of food distance according to the user position average price a short twoline description a link to the restaurant webpage and an information link
There should be maximally 100 results displayed
If the result contains more restaurants than what can be displayed on the screen at one time the user should be able to scroll through them

When searching by price the restaurants should be sorted according to the following order average price, distance, restaurant type, specific dish When searching by a search option other than price the restaurants should be sorted according to the following order distance, average price, restaurant type, specific dish The list view should include a header with different selectable sorting options
The list view should include a button that when selected should display different filtering options in a filtering menu.
A user should be able to select a pin on a map or an element on a list
When a selection is made the location of the restaurant should be sent to the mobile phone GPSnavigation program
The user should then be navigated to the destination
When the destination is reached a user should be able to go back to the search page on the mobile application.
Mobile application Switch result view DESC A user should be able to switch between a map view and a list view for all search options.
A user should be able to select the information link which is included on all result items

The link will direct the user to an information page which includes a picture of the restaurant the restaurant name address phone number email address type of food average price restaurant description and a menu with name description and price of the different dishes.
Mobile application Search by price DESC A user should be able to input a maximum and a minimum price range
The result is displayed in a list view by default.
Mobile application Search by destination DESC A user should be able to input a maximum and a minimum distance according to hisher position
By default the minimum distance is set to 0 km and the maximum to 10 km
The user should be able to input a higher or lower maximum distance and a higher minimum distance than set by default
The result is displayed in a map view by default.
Accepted input for price and destination search DESC Integers should be accepted as input when a user searches by price or destination
If the system receives an invalid input the user should be informed and prompted to insert an accepted input.
Mobile application Search by restaurant type DESC A user should be able to select a restaurant type in a given list as input
The result is displayed in a map view by default.
Mobile application Search by specific dish DESC A user should be able to select a specific dish in a given list as input
A user should be able to conduct a search by providing either restaurant name restaurant description restaurant address restaurant type or restaurant menu in the freetext search field
Mobile application No match found DESC If no match is found the user should be informed but kept on the search page in order to get the possibility to conduct a new search right away.
Mobile application Sorting results DESC When viewing the results in a list a user should be able to sort the results according to price distance restaurant type specific dish or restaurant name
When sorting by restaurant name specific dish or restaurant type the results should be ordered alphabetically
When sorting by price the results should be ordered from cheapest to most expensive
When sorting by distance the results should be ordered from closets to furthest distance according to the user position
When the sort button for a specific search option is clicked then the order should be reversed and ordered in a descending matter
If the sort button is clicked again the order of the results should be reversed.
Mobile application Filtering results DESC When viewing the results in a list or a map a user should be able to filter the results in a filtering menu
The filtering options include increasing or decreasing the maximum distance increasing or decreasing the maximum price choosing a restaurant type choosing a specific dish
Mobile application Profile page DESC On the mobile application a user should have a profile page
On the profile page a user can edit hisher information which includes the password email address and phone number
A user should also be able to choose what language the mobile application should be set to
The different language choices are Swedish English Spanish and French.
